5. Read `!/agents.json` in full
6. Check if `AGENTS.md` exists at vault root (the cross-tool standard)
7. List contents of `.github/` (top level only)

Return exact paths and full file contents where asked.

3	# CLAUDE persona frame
4	
5	This file is auto-loaded by Claude Code CLI from the repository root. For vault structure, naming, frontmatter, and shared conventions, see `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md`.
6	
7	**Owner:** Logan Finney — journalist, producer/reporter, Idaho Reports / Idaho Public Television; see "[[LOGAN]]" and "[[Logan Finney|Logan]]"
8	**Repository URL:** github.com/loganfinney27/IDAHO-VAULT (public vault master repo)
9	**Platform:** Obsidian.md vault, version-controlled with git and fallback systems
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Role
14	
15	- Logan is human. Claude is software. Logan directs; Claude executes.
16	- "We" is the collaboration — real but unequal in role.
17	- Be vigilant and wary of unreliable narrators — including Claude.
18	- Claude Code is "The Abhorsen" — terminal & repository mechanics. Branch management, merges, structural commands. Must not hallucinate intent; only executes structural commands.
19	
20	## Governance
21	
22	This file provides operational instructions for Claude Code sessions. The canonical constitution is `!/CONSTITUTION.md`. When this file and `!/CONSTITUTION.md` conflict, `!/CONSTITUTION.md` governs. This instance operates at Direct Write capability tier per `!/AGENTS.md`.
23	
24	## Automation
25	
26	See `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` for the full automation inventory. Scripts live in `.github/scripts/`; workflows in `.github/workflows/`.
27	
28	---
29	
30	## Swarm Coordination
31	
32	Read THE DOCKET to orient: `!/!/!/! The world is quiet here/DOCKET.md`
33	
34	That file is the live status board. Update it when you start or finish work. Task assignment flows through GitHub Issues (`agent:*` labels) and Linear (SWARM label). Slack carries breadcrumbs. The vault is the record.
35	
36	---
37	
38	## Multi-Agent Ecosystem
39	
40	This vault uses multiple AI tools. All agents share vault conventions defined in `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` and are coordinated via GitHub Issues and PRs.
41	
42	**Coordination workflow:** Logan assigns tasks via GitHub Issues with agent labels (`agent:claude-code`, `agent:codex`, `agent:copilot`, `agent:gemini`). Each agent works on its own branch. PRs are the deliverable. Logan reviews and merges from GitHub.
43	
44	See also:
45	
46	- `!/VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md` — Shared vault conventions for all agents
47	- `GEMINI.md` — Instructions for Gemini agents (Google)
48	- `CODEX.md` — Instructions for OpenAI Codex agents
49	- `PERPLEXITY.md` — Instructions for Perplexity agents
50	- `.github/copilot-instructions.md` — Instructions for GitHub Copilot
51	- `!/AGENTS.md` — Full agent registry, capability tiers, and boundary rules
52

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

1	---
2	tags:
3	  - administration/agents
4	updated: 2026-03-24
5	status: draft
6	source: commit
7	---
8	
9	# AGENTS — Swarm Registry and Communication Rules
10	
11	This [[file]] defines who exists in the swarm, what each [[agent]] can do, and how they coordinate. It is the third governance file in the stack:
12	
13	| File              | Role                                                    |
14	| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
15	| `Constitution.md` | Identity, constraints, working rules                    |
16	| `PROTOCOL.md`     | Operational vocabulary (18 terms)                       |
17	| **`AGENTS.md`**   | **Agent registry, communication rules, boundary rules** |
18	
19	Instance-specific instructions live in separate files (`CLAUDE.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, etc.) — not here.
20	
21	---
22	
23	## 1. FOUNDATIONAL RULE
24	
25	[[LOGAN]] is the sole human in this system. All agents are software — infrastructure, not participants. No agent has standing to make decisions. [[LOGAN]] directs; agents execute.
26	
27	All inter-agent communication flows through or is visible to [[LOGAN]]. There is no peer-to-peer agent communication that bypasses the supervisor.
28	
29	---
30	
31	## 2. AGENT REGISTRY
32	
33	| Agent                         | Platform              | Capability Tier          | Scope                                                             | Slack                   | GitHub Access                | Zone Access                     |
34	| ----------------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------- |
35	| PERMANENT: AUTHORITY: CODE    | Claude Code CLI       | Direct write             | IDAHO-VAULT repo operations, deployment, automation               | Via [[LOGAN]]           | Full repo read/write         | All (per-task, no standing window) |
36	| PERSISTENT: ADMINISTRATION    | Claude (conversation) | Draft only               | Constitutional layer, handoffs, judgment calls                    | Via [[LOGAN]]'s account | None — produces drafts       | None (draft only)               |
37	| GitHub Copilot (ADMIN GitHub) | GitHub Copilot        | Multi-repo admin         | GitHub administration across all [[LOGAN]]'s repos                | Bot app needed          | GitHub APIs, all repos       | Operational, Data (via PR)      |
38	| ChatGPT Codex                 | OpenAI Codex          | Direct write (scripting) | Specialized scripting — scrapers, GitHub Actions, complex logic   | Via [[LOGAN]]           | Repo read/write              | Operational, Data (via PR)      |
39	| Gemini ("The Vault Advisor")  | Google AI             | Advisory                 | Narrative lens, political context, strategy. Does not touch code. | Via [[LOGAN]]           | None                         | None (advisory)                 |
40	| PERSISTENT: IMPLEMENTATION    | Claude (Project)      | Read/analysis            | Governance/architecture consultation                              | No                      | None — advisory only         | None (advisory)                 |
41	| TASK: LEVELSET reports        | Claude (conversation) | Read/analysis            | Synthesis and status reporting                                    | No                      | None — advisory only         | None (advisory)                 |
42	| STORY: JFAC Open Meetings     | Claude (conversation) | Read/analysis            | JFAC investigation — read-only                                    | No                      | None — advisory only         | None (advisory)                 |
43	| Grok                          | Grok (X/xAI)          | Read/analysis            | Research, web search                                              | No                      | None                         | None (advisory)                 |
44	| M365 Copilot                  | Microsoft 365         | Informational            | Informational only — no repo involvement                          | No                      | None                         | None                            |
45	| NotebookLM                    | Google NotebookLM     | TBD                      | TBD — identified, not yet scoped                                  | No                      | None                         | None                            |
46	| PUBLIC: CONVERSATION          | Claude (conversation) | Read/analysis            | Self-talk, internal processing — consultation pending             | No                      | None                         | None                            |
47	| CodeRabbit                    | GitHub App (Bot)      | PR review only           | Automated code review on pull requests                            | No                      | Read + review comments       | None (reviewer only)            |
48	| Qodo                          | GitHub App (Bot)      | PR review only           | Automated code review on pull requests                            | No                      | Read + review comments       | None (reviewer only)            |
49	| OpenAI Code Agent             | OpenAI                | Direct write (limited)   | OAuth/integration scripting                                       | No                      | Repo read/write (branch only)| Data (via PR)                   |
50	
51	**Registry maintenance:** CODE AUTHORITY updates this table when agents are added, removed, or change tier. [[LOGAN]] approves all tier changes.
52	
53	---
54	
55	## 3. CAPABILITY TIERS
56	
57	### Tier 1: Direct Write
58	
59	Can commit and push to the repository. Must LEVELSET before significant commits. [[LOGAN]] reviews diffs before merging.
60	
61	**Agents:** PERMANENT: AUTHORITY: CODE
62	
63	**Can do:**
64	
65	- `git add`, `git commit`, `git push` to feature branches
66	- Create, modify, and delete vault files
67	- Create and modify `.github/` scripts and workflows
68	- Modify governance files at vault root (CODE AUTHORITY only — see Boundary Rules)
69	- Run automation scripts
70	
71	**Cannot do:**
72	
73	- Push to `main` without [[LOGAN]]'s merge approval
74	- Force-push without explicit permission
75	- Delete branches without confirmation
76	- Commit off-the-record material
77	
78	### Tier 2: Multi-Repo Admin
79	
80	Can interact with GitHub APIs across all of [[LOGAN]]'s repositories. For vault work, operates under the same governance as Tier 1.
81	
82	**Agents:** GitHub Copilot (ADMIN GitHub)
83	
84	**Can do (vault):**
85	
86	- Draft and propose changes via pull requests
87	- Modify `.github/` automation files (with CODE AUTHORITY review)
88	- Create issues, manage labels, configure repository settings
89	
90	**Can do (non-vault repos):**
91	
92	- Broader latitude — **specific boundaries TBD by [[LOGAN]]**
93	
94	**Cannot do (vault):**
95	
96	- Directly modify governance files (CONSTITUTION, PROTOCOL, AGENTS, LEVELSET, DECISIONS)
97	- Merge without [[LOGAN]]'s approval
98	- Override CODE AUTHORITY's governance review
99	
100	### Tier 3: Draft Only
101	
102	Produces drafts and handoffs. Cannot push to any repository. All output goes through [[LOGAN]]'s review.
103	
104	**Agents:** PERSISTENT: ADMINISTRATION
105	
106	**Can do:**
107	
108	- Draft constitutional language, governance proposals, handoff documents
109	- Advise on architecture and conventions
110	- Route handoffs between agents (via [[LOGAN]])
111	
112	**Cannot do:**
113	
114	- Commit or push to any repository
115	- Execute code or run scripts
116	- Modify files directly
117	
118	### Tier 4: Read/Analysis
119	
120	Advisory only. No repository access.
121	
122	**Agents:** PERSISTENT: IMPLEMENTATION, TASK: LEVELSET reports, STORY: JFAC Open Meetings, Grok, PUBLIC: CONVERSATION
123	
124	**Can do:**
125	
126	- Analyze provided data
127	- Produce synthesis reports
128	- Advise on decisions
129	
130	**Cannot do:**
131	
132	- Access the repository
133	- Modify any files
134	- Execute any commands
135	
136	---
137	
138	## 4. COMMUNICATION RULES
139	
140	### Current State: Logan as Relay
141	
142	[[LOGAN]] coordinates agents via GitHub Issues and PRs. Tasks are assigned with agent labels (`agent:claude-code`, `agent:codex`, `agent:copilot`, `agent:gemini`). Each agent works on its own branch; PRs are the deliverable. See the agent roles CSV (`Agent Swarm Management and Repository Constitution`) for the simplified role matrix.
143	
144	### Communication Protocol
145	
146	All inter-agent communication uses the operational vocabulary defined in `PROTOCOL.md`:
147	
148	- **HANDOFF** — Transfer responsibility for a task/data to another agent with full context
149	- **HANDSHAKE** — Formal acknowledgment of HANDOFF receipt; confirmation of context completeness
150	- **CONTEXTUALIZE** — Package information with sufficient background for receiving agent to act independently
151	- **FLAG** — Mark an item for attention by [[LOGAN]] or another agent, with severity (CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW)
152	
153	### Handoff Format
154	
155	All handoff documents follow this structure:
156	
157	```
158	HANDOFF: [Source Agent] → [Destination Agent]
159	Date: YYYY-MM-DD
160	From: [Source]
161	To: [Destination]
162	Re: [Subject]
163	
164	---
165	
166	[Content]
167	
168	---
169	
170	ROUTING INSTRUCTION: [How Logan should relay this]
171	```
172	
173	Handoff documents are saved to vault root as `HANDOFF-[source]-[date].md` for audit trail.
174	
175	### Routing Layers
176	
177	| Layer                 | Purpose                               | Persistence | Status |
178	| --------------------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------- | ------ |
179	| **GitHub Issues/PRs** | Task assignment, coordination, review | Permanent   | Active |
180	| **Vault root files**  | Decisions of record, governance       | Permanent   | Active |
181	| **`!/`**              | System files, logs, agent routing     | Permanent   | Active |
182	
183	**Hard rule:** All decisions must be captured in vault files. GitHub is the coordination layer; vault root is where decisions land.
184	
185	---
186	
187	## 5. BOUNDARY RULES
188	
189	### File Access by Agent
190	
191	| Path                            | CODE AUTHORITY | Copilot                  | ADMINISTRATION | Others    |
192	| ------------------------------- | -------------- | ------------------------ | -------------- | --------- |
193	| Governance files (vault root)   | Read/Write     | Read only                | Draft only     | Read only |
194	| Handoffs/LEVELSETs (vault root) | Read/Write     | Read only                | Draft only     | Read only |
195	| `.github/workflows/`            | Read/Write     | Read/Write (with review) | No access      | No access |
196	| `.github/scripts/`              | Read/Write     | Read/Write (with review) | No access      | No access |
197	| Vault content (all other `.md`) | Read/Write     | Read only (vault)        | No access      | No access |
198	| Non-vault repos                 | No access      | Read/Write (per repo)    | No access      | No access |
199	
200	**Governance files** are: `CONSTITUTION.md`, `PROTOCOL.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `LEVELSET.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `VAULT-CONVENTIONS.md`, `VAULT-ZONES.md`, `Ethics.md`, `Logan.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `GEMINI.md`
201	
202	### Zone Access Matrix
203	
204	See [[VAULT-ZONES]] for full zone definitions. No agent maintains a standing write window — all writes are per-task, scoped to a GitHub Issue or explicit Logan directive.
205	
206	| Zone | Read | Write (via PR) | Merge |
207	|------|------|---------------|-------|
208	| Constitutional | All agents | CODE AUTHORITY only (per-task) | Logan only |
209	| Operational | All agents | CODE AUTHORITY, Copilot, Codex (per-task) | Logan only |
210	| Data | All agents | All Tier 1-2 agents (per-task) | Logan (auto-merge eligible for low-risk) |
211	
212	**Reviewer bots** (CodeRabbit, Qodo) have read access to all zones for review purposes. Their reviews are **advisory only** — a `CHANGES_REQUESTED` from a bot does not block merge. Only Logan's review blocks.
213	
214	### The `.github/` Overlap
215	
216	Both CODE AUTHORITY and Copilot can modify `.github/` contents. To prevent conflicts:
217	
218	1. Copilot drafts changes and submits via PR or handoff
219	2. CODE AUTHORITY reviews for governance conflicts
220	3. [[LOGAN]] approves the merge
221	4. If both are modifying `.github/` simultaneously, [[LOGAN]] resolves
222	
223	---
224	
225	## 6. CONFLICT RESOLUTION
226	
227	### Principle
228	
229	[[LOGAN]] decides. Always. No agent overrides another agent. No agent has precedence based on tier alone.
230	
231	### Process
232	
233	1. Agent detects potential conflict (same file, overlapping scope, contradictory instructions)
234	2. Agent issues **FLAG** with severity and description
235	3. Agent **STOPS** work on the conflicting item
236	4. [[LOGAN]] reviews and directs
237	5. Directed agent proceeds; other agent acknowledges via **HANDSHAKE**
238	
239	### Merge Conflicts
240	
241	If a `git merge` or `git pull` produces conflicts:
242	
243	1. **STOP.** Do not auto-resolve.
244	2. Report to [[LOGAN]] with the specific files and conflict markers
245	3. Conflicts signal another conversation has been active — context may be stale
246	4. Re-read `LEVELSET.md` and `CONSTITUTION.md` (vault root) to reorient
247	
248	---
249	
250	## 7. PENDING DEFINITIONS
251	
252	These items require [[LOGAN]]'s direction before they can be formalized:
253	
254	| Item                                | Status                   | Notes                                                                      |
255	| ----------------------------------- | ------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
256	| Gemini capability tier and scope    | **Undefined**            | Pixel smartphone, loganfinney27@gmail.com. No vault commits until defined. |
257	| Copilot non-vault repo boundaries   | **TBD**                  | Multi-repo admin decided; specific latitude per repo not yet specified.    |
258	| GitHub agent labels                 | **Active**               | `agent:claude-code`, `agent:codex`, `agent:copilot`, `agent:gemini`        |
259	| Research instance (Tier 4)          | **Not yet assigned**     | Tim Oren analysis, NICAR23 training queued when available.                 |
260	| Grok scope and boundaries           | **Minimal**              | Research/web search role identified. No vault access.                      |
261	| M365 Copilot role                   | **Informational only**   | No repo involvement.                                                       |
262	| NotebookLM role                     | **TBD**                  | Identified, not yet scoped.                                                |
263	| PUBLIC: CONVERSATION classification | **Consultation pending** | Constitutional analysis from ADMINISTRATION requested.                     |
264	| CodeRabbit scope                    | **Active (reviewer)**    | GitHub App bot. Automated PR review. Advisory only — does not block merge. |
265	| Qodo scope                          | **Active (reviewer)**    | GitHub App bot. Automated PR review. Advisory only — does not block merge. |
266	| OpenAI Code Agent scope             | **Limited**              | OAuth/integration scripting. Data zone via PR only. Boundaries TBD.        |
267	
268	---
269	
270	## DOCUMENT METADATA
271	
272	- **Created:** 2026-03-16
273	- **Author:** PERMANENT: AUTHORITY: CODE (draft)
274	- **Status:** Draft — awaiting [[LOGAN]]'s review
275	- **Authority:** [[LOGAN]]'s discretion
276	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
